revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称=鹿島大神宮 |所在地=[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田村 (福島県)|西田町]]丹伊田字宮作239 |位置={{ウィキ座標2...」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=鹿島大神宮 |所在地=[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田村 (福島県)|西田町]]丹伊田字宮作239 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|28|22.3|N|140|28|27.8|E|scale:5000}} |祭神=[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌大神]] |社格=郷社 |創建=[[天応]]元年（[[781年]]） |例祭=[[4月]]第2土曜日、日曜日 |画像= }} '''鹿島大神宮'''（かしまだいじんぐう）は[[福島県]][[西田村 (福島県)|西田町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[天応]]元年（[[781年]]）[[9月15日]]に、[[鹿島神宮]]（[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]）より勧請したのが始まり。毎年4月の例大祭では、[[神楽|太々神楽]]が奉納されている。
[[天然記念物]]の『ペグマタイト岩脈』で知られる。
== ペグマタイト岩脈 == 境内のあちらこちらに突出する白色の巨大な[[ペグマタイト]]の塊。
かつては、[[阿武隈山地]]は[[ペグマタイト]]の大産出地であったが、ほとんどが工業用原料として採掘され尽くされてしまった。しかし、当地のペグマタイトは神社の[[神体|御神体]]として扱われたことから採掘を免れた。その結果、露出している面の延長は約40m、幅14m、地下10mまではこの岩石が続いており<ref>郡山市教育委員会 ペグマタイト岩脈 案内板</ref>、合計で1万4千[[トン]]もの岩脈があると推定される<ref>[https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html 西田町のみどころ] 郡山市</ref>巨大な岩体が残った。
[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）、国の天然記念物に指定された。
== 交通 == === バス === * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[東北新幹線]][[郡山駅 (福島県)|郡山駅]]6番バス乗り場より堂坂・西田行政センタ経由甲森で約40分「甲森」停留所下車、徒歩6分（ダイヤ注意）
=== 乗用車 === * [[郡山東インターチェンジ|郡山東IC]]から[[福島県道116号二本松三春線]]を経由して約10分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html 郡山市観光協会 - 史跡・公共施設・文化財]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまたいしんくう}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:郡山市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称=鹿島大神宮 |所在地=[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田村 (福島県)|西田町]]丹伊田字宮作239 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|28|22.3|N|140|28|27.8|E|scale:5000}} |祭神=[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌大神]] |社格=郷社 |創建=[[天応]]元年（[[781年]]） |例祭=[[4月]]第2土曜日、日曜日 |画像= }} '''鹿島大神宮'''（かしまだいじんぐう）は[[福島県]][[西田村 (福島県)|西田町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[天応]]元年（[[781年]]）[[9月15日]]に、[[鹿島神宮]]（[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]）より勧請したのが始まり。毎年4月の例大祭では、[[神楽|太々神楽]]が奉納されている。
[[天然記念物]]の『ペグマタイト岩脈』で知られる。
== ペグマタイト岩脈 == 境内のあちらこちらに突出する白色の巨大な[[ペグマタイト]]の塊。
かつては、[[阿武隈山地]]は[[ペグマタイト]]の大産出地であったが、ほとんどが工業用原料として採掘され尽くされてしまった。しかし、当地のペグマタイトは神社の[[神体|御神体]]として扱われたことから採掘を免れた。その結果、露出している面の延長は約40m、幅14m、地下10mまではこの岩石が続いており<ref>郡山市教育委員会 ペグマタイト岩脈 案内板</ref>、合計で1万4千[[トン]]もの岩脈があると推定される<ref>[https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html 西田町のみどころ] 郡山市</ref>巨大な岩体が残った。
[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）、国の天然記念物に指定された。
== 交通 == === バス === * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[東北新幹線]][[郡山駅 (福島県)|郡山駅]]6番バス乗り場より堂坂・西田行政センタ経由甲森で約40分「甲森」停留所下車、徒歩6分（ダイヤ注意）
=== 乗用車 === * [[郡山東インターチェンジ|郡山東IC]]から[[福島県道116号二本松三春線]]を経由して約10分
== 周辺 == * 子和清水 - 郡山市の水とふれあう名所10選。絶え間なく湧き出る清水。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html 郡山市観光協会 - 史跡・公共施設・文化財]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまたいしんくう}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:郡山市の歴史]]

== ペグマタイト岩脈 == 境内のあちらこちらに突出する白色の巨大な[[ペグマタイト]]の塊。
かつては、[[阿武隈山地]]は[[ペグマタイト]]の大産出地であったが、ほとんどが工業用原料として採掘され尽くされてしまった。しかし、当地のペグマタイトは神社の[[神体|御神体]]として扱われたことから採掘を免れた。その結果、露出している面の延長は約40m、幅14m、地下10mまではこの岩石が続いており<ref>郡山市教育委員会 ペグマタイト岩脈 案内板</ref>、合計で1万4千[[トン]]もの岩脈があると推定される<ref>[https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html 西田町のみどころ] 郡山市</ref>巨大な岩体が残った。
[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）、『鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈』として国の天然記念物に指定された。
== 交通 == === バス === * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[東北新幹線]][[郡山駅 (福島県)|郡山駅]]6番バス乗り場より堂坂・西田行政センタ経由甲森で約40分「甲森」停留所下車、徒歩6分（ダイヤ注意）
=== 乗用車 === * [[郡山東インターチェンジ|郡山東IC]]から[[福島県道116号二本松三春線]]を経由して約10分
== 周辺 == * 子和清水 - 郡山市の水とふれあう名所10選。絶え間なく湧き出る清水。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html 郡山市観光協会 - 史跡・公共施設・文化財]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまたいしんくう}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]]

かつては、[[阿武隈山地]]は[[ペグマタイト]]の大産出地であったが、ほとんどが工業用原料として採掘され尽くされてしまった。しかし、当地のペグマタイトは神社の[[神体|御神体]]として扱われたことから採掘を免れた。その結果、露出している面の延長は約40m、幅14m、地下10mまではこの岩石が続いており<ref>郡山市教育委員会 ペグマタイト岩脈 案内板</ref>、合計で1万4千[[トン]]もの岩脈があると推定される<ref>[https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html 西田町のみどころ] 郡山市</ref>巨大な岩体が残った。
[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）、『鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈』として国の天然記念物に指定された。
== 交通 == === バス === * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[東北新幹線]][[郡山駅 (福島県)|郡山駅]]6番バス乗り場より堂坂・西田行政センタ経由甲森で約40分「甲森」停留所下車、徒歩6分（ダイヤ注意）

{{神社 |名称=鹿島大神宮 |所在地=[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]丹伊田字宮作239 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|28|22.3|N|140|28|27.8|E|scale:5000}} |祭神=[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌大神]] |社格=郷社 |創建=[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]） |例祭=[[4月]]第2土曜日、日曜日 |画像= [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]] }} '''鹿島大神宮'''（かしまだいじんぐう）は[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]）[[9月15日]]に、[[鹿島神宮]]（[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]）より勧請したのが始まり。毎年4月の例大祭では、[[神楽|太々神楽]]が奉納されている。

{{DEFAULTSORT:かしまたいしんくう}} [[Category:郡山市の神社]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:福島県の自然景勝地]]

{{神社 |名称=鹿島大神宮 |所在地=[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]丹伊田字宮作239 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|28|22.3|N|140|28|27.8|E|scale:5000}} |祭神=[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌大神]] |社格=郷社 |創建=[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]） |例祭=[[4月]]第2土曜日、日曜日 |画像= [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]] }} '''鹿島大神宮'''（かしまだいじんぐう）は[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]）[[9月15日]]に、[[鹿島神宮]]（[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]）より勧請したのが始まり。毎年4月の例大祭では、[[神楽|太々神楽]]が奉納されている。
[[天然記念物]]の『ペグマタイト岩脈』で知られる。
== ペグマタイト岩脈 == 境内のあちらこちらに突出する白色の巨大な[[ペグマタイト]]の塊。
かつては、[[阿武隈山地]]は[[ペグマタイト]]の大産出地であったが、ほとんどが工業用原料として採掘され尽くされてしまった。しかし、当地のペグマタイトは神社の[[神体|御神体]]として扱われたことから採掘を免れた。その結果、露出している面の延長は約40m、幅14m、地下10mまではこの岩石が続いており<ref>郡山市教育委員会 ペグマタイト岩脈 案内板</ref>、合計で1万4千[[トン]]もの岩脈があると推定される<ref>[https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html 西田町のみどころ] 郡山市</ref>巨大な岩体が残った。
[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）、『鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈』として国の天然記念物に指定された。
== 交通 == === 乗用車 === * [[郡山東インターチェンジ|郡山東IC]]から[[福島県道116号二本松三春線]]を経由して約10分
== 周辺 == * 子和清水 - 郡山市の水とふれあう名所10選。絶え間なく湧き出る清水。 * [[デコ屋敷|高柴デコ屋敷]] - [[三春駒]]や[[三春張子人形]]の発祥地。土産物店や絵付け体験ができる工房などあり。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Pegmatite dike at Kashima shrine}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html 郡山市観光協会 - 史跡・公共施設・文化財] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ 鹿島大神宮 – 福島県郡山市西田町 ペグマタイト]
{{Shinto-stub}}

[[天然記念物]]の「ペグマタイト岩脈」で知られる。
== ペグマタイト岩脈 == 境内のあちらこちらに突出する白色の巨大な[[ペグマタイト]]の塊。
かつては、[[阿武隈山地]]は[[ペグマタイト]]の大産出地であったが、ほとんどが工業用原料として採掘され尽くされてしまった。しかし、当地のペグマタイトは神社の[[神体|御神体]]として扱われたことから採掘を免れた。その結果、露出している面の延長は約40[[メートル]]、幅14メートル、地下10メートルまではこの岩石が続いており<ref>郡山市教育委員会 ペグマタイト岩脈 案内板</ref>、合計で1万4千[[トン]]もの岩脈があると推定される{{r|西田町のみどころ}}巨大な岩体が残った。
[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）、「鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈」として国の天然記念物に指定された。
== 交通 == === 乗用車 === * [[郡山東インターチェンジ|郡山東IC]]から[[福島県道116号二本松三春線]]を経由して約10分
== 周辺 == * 子和清水 - 郡山市の水とふれあう名所10選。絶え間なく湧き出る清水。 * [[デコ屋敷|高柴デコ屋敷]] - [[三春駒]]や[[三春張子人形]]の発祥地。土産物店や絵付け体験ができる工房などあり。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="西田町のみどころ">{{cite web | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = 西田町のみどころ | publisher = 郡山市 | date = 2014-2-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-8 | deadlinkdate = 2022年10月21日 }}</ref> }}
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html 郡山市観光協会 - 史跡・公共施設・文化財] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ 鹿島大神宮 – 福島県郡山市西田町 ペグマタイト]
{{shinto-stub}}
{{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈 |1-1=地質・鉱物天然記念物 |1-2=福島県にある国指定の天然記念物 |1-3=郡山市の地理 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまたいしんくう}} [[Category:郡山市の神社]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:福島県の自然景勝地]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=鹿島大神宮 |所在地=[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]丹伊田字宮作239 |位置={{Coord|37|28|22.3|N|140|28|27.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌大神]] |社格=郷社 |創建=[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]） |例祭=[[4月]]第2土曜日、日曜日 |画像=[[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=鹿島大神宮|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''鹿島大神宮'''（かしまだいじんぐう）は[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]）[[9月15日]]に、[[鹿島神宮]]（[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]）より勧請したのが始まり。毎年4月の例大祭では、[[神楽|太々神楽]]が奉納されている。
[[天然記念物]]の「ペグマタイト岩脈」で知られる。

[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）、「鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈」として国の天然記念物に指定された。 {{main|鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈}}
== 交通 == === 乗用車 === * [[郡山東インターチェンジ|郡山東IC]]から[[福島県道116号二本松三春線]]を経由して約10分
== 周辺 == * 子和清水 - 郡山市の水とふれあう名所10選。絶え間なく湧き出る清水。 * [[デコ屋敷|高柴デコ屋敷]] - [[三春駒]]や[[三春張子人形]]の発祥地。土産物店や絵付け体験ができる工房などあり。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="西田町のみどころ">{{cite web | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = 西田町のみどころ | publisher = 郡山市 | date = 2014-2-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-8 | deadlinkdate = 2022年10月21日 }}</ref> }}
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html 郡山市観光協会 - 史跡・公共施設・文化財] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ 鹿島大神宮 – 福島県郡山市西田町 ペグマタイト]
{{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称=鹿島大神宮 |画像=[[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]丹伊田字宮作239 |位置={{Coord|37|28|22.3|N|140|28|27.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌大神]] |社格=旧[[郷社]] |創建=[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]） |例祭=[[4月]]第2土曜日、日曜日 |地図={{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=鹿島大神宮|position=top|float=center|relief=|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|37|28|22.3|N|140|28|27.8|E}}}} }} '''鹿島大神宮'''（かしまだいじんぐう）は[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]）[[9月15日]]に、[[鹿島神宮]]（[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]）より勧請したのが始まり。毎年4月の例大祭では、[[神楽|太々神楽]]が奉納されている。 [[天然記念物]]の「ペグマタイト岩脈」で知られる。 == ペグマタイト岩脈 == 境内のあちらこちらに突出する白色の巨大な[[ペグマタイト]]の塊。 かつては、[[阿武隈山地]]は[[ペグマタイト]]の大産出地であったが、ほとんどが工業用原料として採掘され尽くされてしまった。しかし、当地のペグマタイトは神社の[[神体|御神体]]として扱われたことから採掘を免れた。その結果、露出している面の延長は約40[[メートル]]、幅14メートル、地下10メートルまではこの岩石が続いており郡山市教育委員会 ペグマタイト岩脈 案内板、合計で1万4千[[トン]]もの岩脈があると推定される{{r|西田町のみどころ}}巨大な岩体が残った。 [[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）、「鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈」として国の天然記念物に指定された。 {{main|鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈}} == 交通 == === 乗用車 === * [[郡山東インターチェンジ|郡山東IC]]から[[福島県道116号二本松三春線]]を経由して約10分 == 周辺 == * 子和清水 - 郡山市の水とふれあう名所10選。絶え間なく湧き出る清水。 * [[デコ屋敷|高柴デコ屋敷]] - [[三春駒]]や[[三春張子人形]]の発祥地。土産物店や絵付け体験ができる工房などあり。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = 西田町のみどころ | publisher = 郡山市 | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html 郡山市観光協会 - 史跡・公共施設・文化財] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ 鹿島大神宮 – 福島県郡山市西田町 ペグマタイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまたいしんくう}} [[Category:郡山市の神社]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:福島県の自然景勝地]]

{{Shrine |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |address = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. Founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == Festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s Festival * [[April]]: Annual Festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification Ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest Festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[Miharu horse figurines]] and [[Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Daijingu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kashima shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]]

In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: annual festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[Miharu horse figurines]] and [[Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Daijingu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kashima shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]]

This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[Miharu horse figurines]] and [[Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Daijingu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kashima shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]]

Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Daijingu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kashima shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]]

拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[Miharu horse figurines]] and [[Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. 拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[Miharu horse figurines]] and [[Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Daijingu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kashima shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. 拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories|}} {{draft categories|}} {{draft categories|}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kashima shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Kashima Shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[en:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Daijingu}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)}}

In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kashima shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Kashima Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[en:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Daijingu}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kashima shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:Kashima Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[en:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Daijingu}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)}}

revision.comment: 28 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市) up to rev 101775408
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=鹿島大神宮 |画像=[[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]丹伊田字宮作239 |位置={{Coord|37|28|22.3|N|140|28|27.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌大神]] |社格=旧[[郷社]] |創建=[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]） |例祭=[[4月]]第2土曜日、日曜日 |地図={{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=鹿島大神宮|position=top|float=center|relief=|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|37|28|22.3|N|140|28|27.8|E}}}} }} '''鹿島大神宮'''（かしまだいじんぐう）は[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]）[[9月15日]]に、[[鹿島神宮]]（[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]）より勧請したのが始まり。毎年4月の例大祭では、[[神楽|太々神楽]]が奉納されている。 [[天然記念物]]の「ペグマタイト岩脈」で知られる。 == ペグマタイト岩脈 == 境内のあちらこちらに突出する白色の巨大な[[ペグマタイト]]の塊。 かつては、[[阿武隈山地]]は[[ペグマタイト]]の大産出地であったが、ほとんどが工業用原料として採掘され尽くされてしまった。しかし、当地のペグマタイトは神社の[[神体|御神体]]として扱われたことから採掘を免れた。その結果、露出している面の延長は約40[[メートル]]、幅14メートル、地下10メートルまではこの岩石が続いており郡山市教育委員会 ペグマタイト岩脈 案内板、合計で1万4千[[トン]]もの岩脈があると推定される{{r|西田町のみどころ}}巨大な岩体が残った。 [[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）、「鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈」として国の天然記念物に指定された。 {{main|鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈}} == 交通 == === 乗用車 === * [[郡山東インターチェンジ|郡山東IC]]から[[福島県道116号二本松三春線]]を経由して約10分 == 周辺 == * 子和清水 - 郡山市の水とふれあう名所10選。絶え間なく湧き出る清水。 * [[デコ屋敷|高柴デコ屋敷]] - [[三春駒]]や[[三春張子人形]]の発祥地。土産物店や絵付け体験ができる工房などあり。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = 西田町のみどころ | publisher = 郡山市 | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html 郡山市観光協会 - 史跡・公共施設・文化財] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ 鹿島大神宮 – 福島県郡山市西田町 ペグマタイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまたいしんくう}} [[Category:郡山市の神社]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:福島県の自然景勝地]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=鹿島大神宮 |画像=[[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]丹伊田字宮作239 |位置={{Coord|37|28|22.3|N|140|28|27.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌大神]] |社格=旧[[郷社]] |創建=[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]） |例祭=[[4月]]第2土曜日、日曜日 |地図={{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=鹿島大神宮|position=top|float=center|relief=|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|37|28|22.3|N|140|28|27.8|E}}}} }} '''鹿島大神宮'''（かしまだいじんぐう）は[[福島県]][[郡山市]][[西田町 (郡山市)|西田町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]）[[9月15日]]に、[[鹿島神宮]]（[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]）より勧請したのが始まり。毎年4月の例大祭では、[[神楽|太々神楽]]が奉納されている。 [[天然記念物]]の「ペグマタイト岩脈」で知られる。 == ペグマタイト岩脈 == 境内のあちらこちらに突出する白色の巨大な[[ペグマタイト]]の塊。 かつては、[[阿武隈山地]]は[[ペグマタイト]]の大産出地であったが、ほとんどが工業用原料として採掘され尽くされてしまった。しかし、当地のペグマタイトは神社の[[神体|御神体]]として扱われたことから採掘を免れた。その結果、露出している面の延長は約40[[メートル]]、幅14メートル、地下10メートルまではこの岩石が続いており郡山市教育委員会 ペグマタイト岩脈 案内板、合計で1万4千[[トン]]もの岩脈があると推定される{{r|西田町のみどころ}}巨大な岩体が残った。 [[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）、「鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈」として国の天然記念物に指定された。 {{main|鹿島神社のペグマタイト岩脈}} == 交通 == === 乗用車 === * [[郡山東インターチェンジ|郡山東IC]]から[[福島県道116号二本松三春線]]を経由して約10分 == 周辺 == * 子和清水 - 郡山市の水とふれあう名所10選。絶え間なく湧き出る清水。 * [[デコ屋敷|高柴デコ屋敷]] - [[三春駒]]や[[三春張子人形]]の発祥地。土産物店や絵付け体験ができる工房などあり。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = 西田町のみどころ | publisher = 郡山市 | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html 郡山市観光協会 - 史跡・公共施設・文化財] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ 鹿島大神宮 – 福島県郡山市西田町 ペグマタイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまたいしんくう}} [[Category:郡山市の神社]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:福島県の自然景勝地]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kashima shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:r]] [[Category:Kashima Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[en:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Daijingu}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)}} {{translated page|ja|鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|version=101775408|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|version=101775408|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kashima shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:r]] [[Category:Kashima Shrines]] }} [[sn:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[en:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]]

拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|version=101775408|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kashima shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:r]] [[Category:Kashima Shrines]] }} [[sn:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:福島県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:Shrines in Koriyama�]] {{Commons category|Kashima Daijingu}}
