{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[:en:781 CE|781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Jingū]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[:en:Abukuma Mountains|Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|version=101775408|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:r]] }} [[sn:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:福島県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:Shrines in Koriyama]] {{Commons category|Kashima Daijingu}}

拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[:en:781 CE|781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[natural monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[:en:Abukuma Mountains|Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[:en:April|April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[:en:June 30|June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|version=101775408|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:福島県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:Shrines in Koriyama]] {{Commons category|Kashima Daijingu}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:福島県の自然景勝地]] into [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Fukushima Prefecture]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = [[File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[:en:781 CE|781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature. == Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[:en:pegmatite|pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tonsEducational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[:en:Abukuma Mountains|Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved. In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}} == festivals and Events == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[:en:April|April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[:en:June 30|June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[:en:November|November]]: Harvest festival == Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]]. == Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|version=101775408|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Koriyama]] {{Commons category|Kashima Daijingu}}

Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }} }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|version=101775408|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Kōriyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] [[ja:鹿島大神宮]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Koriyama]] {{Commons category|Kashima Daijingu}} [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kashima Daijingu]][[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28687197]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28687197]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima |location = {{ill|Kōriyama|qid=Q264206}} |coordinates = {{Coord|37.47286111111111|N|140.47438888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[:en:781 CE|781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature.
== Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[:en:pegmatite|pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tons<ref>Educational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.</ref>. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[:en:Abukuma Mountains|Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}}
== festivals and Events == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[:en:April|April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[:en:June 30|June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[:en:November|November]]: Harvest festival
== Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]].
== Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting.
== Notes ==
{{reflist|refs= <ref name="nishidamidoroko">{{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }}</ref> }}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kashima Daijingu]][[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28687197]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28687197]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima |location = {{ill|Kōriyama|WD=Q264206}} |coordinates = {{Coord|37.47286111111111|N|140.47438888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[:en:781 CE|781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature.
== Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[:en:pegmatite|pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tons<ref>Educational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.</ref>. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[:en:Abukuma Mountains|Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}}
== festivals and Events == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[:en:April|April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[:en:June 30|June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[:en:November|November]]: Harvest festival
== Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]].
== Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting.
== Notes ==
{{reflist|refs= <ref name="nishidamidoroko">{{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }}</ref> }}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site]

'''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[:en:781 CE|781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature.
== Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[:en:pegmatite|pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tons<ref>Educational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.</ref>. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[:en:Abukuma Mountains|Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}}
== festivals and Events == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[:en:April|April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[:en:June 30|June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[:en:November|November]]: Harvest festival
== Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]].
== Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting.
== Notes ==
{{reflist|refs= <ref name="nishidamidoroko">{{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }}</ref> }}

'''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[:en:781 CE|781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature.
== Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[:en:pegmatite|pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tons<ref>Educational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.</ref>. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[:en:Abukuma Mountains|Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}}
== festivals and Events == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[:en:April|April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[:en:June 30|June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[:en:November|November]]: Harvest festival
== Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]].
== Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting.
== Notes ==
{{reflist|refs= <ref name="nishidamidoroko">{{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }}</ref> }}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kashima Daijingu]][[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kashima Daijingu]][[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kashima Daijingu]][[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kashima Daijingu]][[ja:鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28687197]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28687197]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima |location = {{ill|Kōriyama|WD=Q264206}} |coordinates = {{Coord|37.47286111111111|N|140.47438888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], Japan. founded in [[:en:781 CE|781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature.
== Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of [[:en:pegmatite|pegmatite]], forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tons<ref>Educational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.</ref>. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[:en:Abukuma Mountains|Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}}
== festivals and Events == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: New Year’s festival * [[:en:April|April]]: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * [[:en:June 30|June 30]]: Great Purification ceremony * [[:en:November|November]]: Harvest festival
== Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]].
== Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of [[:en:Miharu-koma|Miharu horse figurines]] and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting.
== Notes ==
{{reflist|refs= <ref name="nishidamidoroko">{{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }}</ref> }}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima |location = {{ill|Kōriyama|WD=Q264206}} |coordinates = {{Coord|37.47286111111111|N|140.47438888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, Japan. founded in [[:en:781 CE|781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū|Kashima Jingū]] in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. The shrine is renowned for its [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature.
== Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of {{ill|pegmatite|en|pegmatite|WD=Q105630|lt=pegmatite}}, forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tons<ref>Educational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.</ref>. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[:en:Abukuma Mountains|Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved.
In {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}}
== festivals and Events == * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}: New Year’s festival * {{ill|April|en|April|WD=Q118|lt=April}}: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}}: Great Purification ceremony * {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November}}: Harvest festival
== Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]].
== Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of {{ill|Miharu-koma|en|Miharu-koma|WD=Q6845257|lt=Miharu horse figurines}} and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting.
== Notes ==
{{reflist|refs= <ref name="nishidamidoroko">{{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }}</ref> }}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kashima Daijingū |image = File:鹿島大神宮(郡山市) 拝殿 20170729.jpg |location = 239 Miyazukuri, Nitta, Nishida-machi, Kōriyama City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan |latitude_deg = 37 |latitude_min = 28 |latitude_sec = 22.3 |longitude_deg = 140 |longitude_min = 28 |longitude_sec = 27.8 |deity = [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]] |Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) |founded = [[Ten'ō]] 1 (781 CE) |annual_festival = Second Saturday and Sunday of April |map = Japan Fukushima |location = {{ill|Kōriyama|WD=Q264206|1=Kōriyama}} |coordinates = {{Coord|37.47286111111111|N|140.47438888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kashima Daijingū''' ({{nihongo|鹿島大神宮|かしまだいじんぐう}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Nishida-machi]], [[Kōriyama City]], {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, Japan. founded in [[:en:781 CE|781 CE]], it enshrines the deity [[Takemikazuchi-no-Ōkami]], brought from [[Kashima Jingū|Kashima Jingū]] in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. The shrine is renowned for its [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]], the "Pegmatite Dike," a rare geological feature.
== Pegmatite Dike == The shrine grounds feature large, protruding white blocks of {{ill|pegmatite|en|pegmatite|WD=Q105630|lt=pegmatite|1=Pegmatite}}, forming a massive dike. This geological formation spans approximately 40 meters in length, 14 meters in width, and 10 meters below ground, with an estimated total mass of 14,000 tons<ref>Educational Signage, Kōriyama City Board of Education.</ref>. Pegmatite deposits were once abundant in the [[:en:Abukuma Mountains|Abukuma Mountains]], but most were quarried for industrial purposes. Due to their designation as sacred objects at Kashima Daijingū, the pegmatite here has been preserved.
In {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} (Showa 41), the "Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine" was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] of Japan. {{main|Pegmatite Dike of Kashima Shrine}}
== festivals and Events == * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}}: New Year’s festival * {{ill|April|en|April|WD=Q118|lt=April|1=April}}: festival (Second Saturday and Sunday) – Includes performances of [[Daidai Kagura]]. * {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30|1=June 30}}: Great Purification ceremony * {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November|1=November}}: Harvest festival
== Transportation == === By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes from the [[Kōriyama-higashi Interchange]] on the [[Ban'etsu Expressway]] via the [[Fukushima Prefectural Route 116]].
== Nearby Attractions == * '''Koazu Spring''' – Selected as one of Kōriyama’s "Ten Scenic Water Spots," offering a steady flow of pristine spring water. * '''Takashiba Dekoyashiki''' – The birthplace of {{ill|Miharu-koma|en|Miharu-koma|WD=Q6845257|lt=Miharu horse figurines|1=Miharu-koma}} and [[:ja:三春張子人形|Miharu papier-mâché dolls]]. Features souvenir shops and workshops where visitors can try painting.
== Notes ==
{{reflist|refs= <ref name="nishidamidoroko">{{Cite web|和書 | url = https://www.city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20171108201504/city.koriyama.fukushima.jp/457000/gaiyo/midokoro.html | title = Nishida Town Highlights | publisher = Kōriyama City | date = 2014-02-26 | accessdate = 2016-11-22 | archivedate = 2017-11-08 | url-status=dead|url-status-date=2022-10-21 }}</ref> }}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kashima Daijingu}} * [http://www.kanko-koriyama.gr.jp/tourism/detail2-0-71.html Kōriyama Tourism Association – Historical Sites & Cultural Properties] * [https://kashimadaijingu.jp/ Kashima Daijingū – Official Site]
